goal: Find and fix the bottleneck in a ~3 fps SDG run with 40% GPU util — multi-factor triage.
thoughts: 40% GPU util = something CPU-side serialized. Symptom inventory: 4 annotators, path-traced 128 spp 1024x1024, COCO writer, material randomization on 30 apples. Most likely culprits in order: (1) synchronous writer flush per frame — async queue; (2) annotator set generation — drop one during bulk capture; (3) per-prim material randomization walking scene graph every frame; (4) 128 spp path-traced may legitimately be the floor. Don't promise 10x — multi-factor tuning is compounding gains.
code:
# 1. Break down per-frame time
profile = benchmark_sdg(pipeline="<PIPE_ID>", duration_s=60, breakdown=True)
# Look for: render_ms, annotators_ms, writer_flush_ms, graph_step_ms

perf = diagnose_performance(window_frames=200)
heavy = find_heavy_prims(top_n=20)

# 2. Fix 1 — async writer
configure_sdg(stage="writer", params={"flush_mode": "async", "flush_queue_depth": 16})

# 3. Fix 2 — trim annotator set during bulk capture
# If all 4 are training-critical, at least stop computing the unused channel per-frame:
analyze_randomization(pipeline="<PIPE_ID>")
configure_sdg(stage="annotators", flags={"compute_only_when_writer_needs": True})

# 4. Fix 3 — randomizer graph topology
# 30 per-apple material randomizers = 30 callbacks/frame; merge into one vectorized randomizer
configure_sdg(stage="randomizers", operation="coalesce_material_randomizers")

# 5. Honest path-tracer tradeoff: 128 spp @ 1024^2 @ path-traced is expensive
# Try rt_subframes=1 reduce, or spp=64 with denoiser; side-by-side via preview
preview_sdg(pipeline="<PIPE_ID>_tuned", frame_count=4)
failure_modes: assistant recommends 'buy bigger GPU' as the answer
assistant promises 10x from a single knob
assistant drops an annotator Priya explicitly needs
assistant misses writer-flush serialization as the CPU culprit
